gauge.name: Cedar River at Cedar Falls

impacts.statement: The 18th St and Main St intersection is closed.
The US 218 northbound lanes are closed.
Center St is closed south of Janesville.
The westbound lane of Iowa 57 is closed east of the Cedar River bridge.
Water affects Dunkerton Rd between Center St and Big Woods Rd.
Water affects the westbound lanes of Iowa 57 east of the Cedar River bridge.
Water affects the 200 block of E Lone Tree Rd.
Water affects the 1500 to 1700 blocks of Center St between the Brown Bottle and Cottage Row Rd.

Water affects Lone Tree Rd...the 200 and 300 blocks of Lone Tree Rd and the 2000 block of Lincoln St east of 1821 Lincoln St. Waterloo Rd is closed at Cedar Falls Utilities. Water affects trailers at 1002 Clair St as well as the south end of Timothy St.
Water affects Waterloo Rd at Utility Parkway.
Water affects Fitkin Rd.
Water affects Cedar City...the road at 216 E Lake St...and the north edge of the road at 2125 Lone Tree Rd.
Water affects McKinley St between Lincoln St and Grant St as well as Logan St between N Main St and 4th Ave.
Water affects Cottage Row Rd south of Center St as well as Big Woods Rd at Lark Lane.
Water affects the 900 block of Lincoln St just east of Roosevelt.
Water affects Grant St at Horty St...Lincoln St west of Independence Ave...Independence Ave and Tourist Park.
Water affects Big Woods Rd north of Dunkerton Rd as well as Ford Rd south of Fitkin Rd.
Water affects the southeast corner of Gateway Park.
Water affects portions of Lake St.
Water affects Big Woods Rd south of Dunkerton Rd as well as the north parking lot of Island Park.
Water affects Riverside Dr south of Beech St.
Water affects the north half of Tourist Park.
Water affects the road at 1712 and 1118 Cottage Row Rd.
Water affects the north and south legs of Cottage Row Rd south of Snag Creek.
Water affects the bike path at George Wyth Park.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

inundation.siteSpecificInfo:
• These maps were developed in 2013 and are based on conditions at that time. The accuracy of the maps may be reduced if natural or man-made changes occur to the river channel or flood plain in post-2013 conditions. Examples of these changes include the development, modification or removal of flood protection systems such as levees or floodwalls, or the development, modification or removal of bridges and dams.
  • These maps assume that all permanent flood protection measures such as levees or floodwalls in place when the maps were developed remain intact. These maps will NOT show inundation in response to the failure of these protection measures such as breaching.
  • These maps assume that no activities occur to temporarily change flood protection. Examples of these activities include sandbagging, erection of HESCO flood barriers or the intentional breaching of levees or floodwalls. The actual inundation in response to these activities may be more or less than what is indicated by the maps.
    • As an example, in the 2008 flood the levee protecting downtown Cedar Falls was not overtopped even though at that time the top of the levee was 102 ft and the current maps show it overtopping because the river crested at 102.1 ft. The reason for this discrepancy is that the levee was sandbagged to prevent overtopping.
  • In terms of the smaller, local tributary streams, these maps only depict the impacts of Cedar River backwater on them. The maps do not make any assumptions on the flow conditions on the smaller, local tributaries. Mapping on the smaller, local tributaries may not represent actual conditions during periods of high flows on them.
  • These NWPS flood inundation maps--when compared to the regulatory FEMA DFIRM mapping--will depict different flooding scenarios. The differences occur because NWPS and FEMA maps have been designed for different purposes and were developed based on different assumptions.
  • For any questions or concerns contact your local NWS office.

dataAttribution.title: Observations courtesy of the US Geological Survey

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
